title: Vibrant Crispy Ethiopian Mung Bean Sprouts Injera
description: A Whole30-friendly Ethiopian dish featuring crispy mung bean sprouts on a bed of injera, perfect for a romantic dinner
instructions: Rinse the mung bean sprouts and blanch them in boiling water for 2 minutes
Drain the mung bean sprouts and set them aside to cool
In a large bowl, mix together the teff flour and water to form a smooth batter
Heat a non-stick skillet or injera pan over medium heat and brush with coconut oil
Pour about 1/4 cup of the batter onto the skillet and tilt to evenly coat the bottom
Cook the injera for 1-2 minutes, until the edges start to curl and the surface is dry
Repeat the process to make about 8-10 injera
In a separate pan, heat some coconut oil over medium heat and sauté the garlic and onion until softened
Add the cooled mung bean sprouts to the pan and stir to combine with the garlic and onion
Season with salt and black pepper to taste
To serve, place a few pieces of injera on a plate and top with the crispy mung bean sprouts and garnish with chopped cilantro
